name: ghe-transition
description: Change phases in the GitHub Elements workflow by closing current thread and opening the next. Use for DEV→TEST, TEST→REVIEW, REVIEW→merge transitions, or demotions. Do not use for saving progress.
capabilities: Validate phase transition prerequisites using the phase-gate agent
Close current thread and open next phase thread in GitHub
Manage demotions between phases (TEST to DEV, REVIEW to DEV)
Handle final merge after REVIEW passes
Sync memory bank state during phase transitions
Respect enforcement level settings from configuration
author: Emasoft
securitySummary: This skill is pure documentation with no executable code. All 15 static findings are false positives caused by pattern-matching on markdown documentation text. The skill describes a legitimate workflow management system for GitHub Elements phase transitions.
valueStatement: Managing multi-phase development workflows requires manually tracking and closing threads. This skill automates phase transitions in GitHub Elements, closing the current thread and opening the next phase thread with proper validation.
limitations: Requires GitHub Elements workflow to be set up in the project
Cannot execute transitions without proper permissions on the GitHub repository
Does not create new issues from scratch, only transitions existing workflow threads
Does not provide status reports (use ghe-status for that)

useCases.targetUser: Developers who finished coding and need to move work to testing
useCases.title: Advance from DEV to TEST phase
useCases.description: Complete development work and transition the issue thread to the TEST phase for quality validation.

useCases.targetUser: QA engineers or developers verifying test completion
useCases.title: Move from TEST to REVIEW phase
useCases.description: After tests pass, transition the thread to REVIEW phase for final code review and merge approval.

useCases.targetUser: QA engineers or reviewers who find blocking issues
useCases.title: Demote issues back to DEV
useCases.description: When structural issues are found during TEST or REVIEW, demote the issue back to DEV for rework.

promptTemplates.title: Basic phase transition
promptTemplates.prompt: I'm done with DEV, transition this issue to TEST phase.
promptTemplates.scenario: Standard transition from development to testing

promptTemplates.title: Demotion request
promptTemplates.prompt: We found structural issues that need rework. Please demote this back to DEV.
promptTemplates.scenario: Moving an issue backwards due to blocking problems

promptTemplates.title: Final merge transition
promptTemplates.prompt: Review passed. This is ready to merge. Please complete the transition.

promptTemplates.title: Complete workflow transition
promptTemplates.prompt: All tests are passing and this is ready for review. Move it to REVIEW phase.
promptTemplates.scenario: Transition from testing to review phase

bestPractices: Use ghe-checkpoint to save progress if you want to continue working in the same phase
Ensure completion comments exist before attempting transitions
Verify tests are passing before transitioning to REVIEW phase
antiPatterns: Using ghe-transition just to check status (use ghe-status instead)
Transitioning without ensuring prerequisites are met
Using this skill to create new issues from scratch (use ghe-claim instead)

faq.question: What is the difference between ghe-transition and ghe-checkpoint?
faq.answer: ghe-transition closes the current phase thread and opens the next phase thread. ghe-checkpoint saves progress but keeps you in the same phase.

faq.question: Can I transition from DEV directly to REVIEW?
faq.answer: No. Transitions must follow the workflow: DEV to TEST, then TEST to REVIEW. Direct transitions are blocked.

faq.question: What happens if phase-gate validation fails?
faq.answer: The transition is blocked. The skill reports why validation failed and you must address the issues before retrying.

faq.question: Does this skill work with any GitHub issue?
faq.answer: No. The issue must be part of the GitHub Elements workflow system with proper labels and thread structure.

faq.question: Can I transition from REVIEW back to TEST?
faq.answer: No. If REVIEW fails, you must demote to DEV, not TEST. This prevents skipping validation steps.

faq.question: How does memory sync work during transition?
faq.answer: The memory-sync agent updates the SERENA memory bank, moving the issue from activeContext.md to progress.md and preparing for the new thread.
